data.content_ref: inline:{"claim":"The abrupt termination of tin supply from Central Asian networks after 1180 BCE precipitated a bronze shortage that crippled elite armament production.","mechanism":"Supply‑chain collapse of tin leading to weapon scarcity, eroding palace military power and administrative cohesion.","scope":{"covers":["Mycenaean Greece","Hittite Anatolia","Ugarit","Levantine city‑states"],"excludes":["Egypt"]},"forbidden":[{"case":"If isotopic analyses of Late Bronze Age bronze artefacts continue to show tin sourced from Anatolian mines post‑1150 BCE","eval":"rubric:std-hist"}],"prose_notes":"Quantitative modelling of commodity flow demonstrates a critical threshold where tin deficit translates into rapid sociopolitical decay, an angle less explored in existing literature."}
